subject: Civics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the purpose and importance of the Constitution
Evaluate why democratic countries need a constitution
Understand the historical process of drafting the Indian Constitution
Examine key features such as federalism, parliamentary system and separation of powers
Analyse the role of Fundamental Rights in protecting citizens
Evaluate how constitutional values promote democracy and social justice

lesson_plan.title: Concept and Purpose of a Constitution
topics_covered: Functions of a constitution in a democratic state
student_activities: Students analyse how rules influence behaviour in institutions
Write a short explanation of why a country needs a constitution

lesson_plan.title: Historical Context of the Indian Constitution
topics_covered: Debates and discussions during constitution making
Adoption of the Constitution on 26 January 1950
student_activities: Create a timeline of events related to constitution making
Discuss why multiple viewpoints were important during drafting

lesson_plan.title: Key Features of the Indian Constitution
student_activities: Draw a diagram showing the division of powers
Explain how federalism helps govern a large country

lesson_plan.title: Role of Parliament and Government
topics_covered: Accountability of the executive to Parliament
student_activities: Analyse how the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers are accountable to Parliament
Discuss the importance of representation in democracy

lesson_plan.title: Fundamental Rights and Constitutional Values
topics_covered: Examples such as Right to Equality and Freedom
student_activities: Analyse a scenario where citizens’ rights are violated
Explain how the Constitution protects these rights

lesson_plan.title: Constitutional Values and Chapter Review
topics_covered: Importance of constitutional values in democracy
student_activities: Write a short reflection on the importance of constitutional values
Participate in a quiz covering the entire chapter

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect constitutional principles such as equality and justice with real-life social situations to deepen their understanding of democratic governance.
